texto: ...first, in order to remember, something must be forgotten; second, the place where memories are stored has no boundaries. In other words, forgetting is a twin; its tandem effect is best called "simultancous" distraction, the instant when one memory defoliates another. This fuzzy double - one devouring the other - presumably inhibits learning
explicacao: Orientação direta de Norman Klein sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “...first, in order to remember, something must be forgotten; second, the place where memories are…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, docufables, forget, útil em momentos de forget e forgetting.
